ddls:
CREATE TABLE "frames" (
  "frame_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frame */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session in which the frame was captured */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the frame was captured */,
  "frame_number" INTEGER /* Sequential number of the frame within the session */,
  "frame_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the frame capture (in milliseconds) */,
  "frame_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the frame (e.g., resolution, lighting conditions) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frame_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_frames_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who conducted the session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of session (e.g., rehabilitation, sports training) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the session was conducted */,
  "device_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the device used to capture the session */,
  "session_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the session */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient (if applicable) for healthcare scenarios */,
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the coach or trainer (if applicable) for sports scenarios */,
  "game_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the game (if applicable) for gaming scenarios */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_session_type FOREIGN KEY ("session_type") REFERENCES session_types ("session_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., physiotherapist, sports trainer) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the user */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the user (e.g., orthopedics, cardiology) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "session_types" (
  "session_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session type */,
  "session_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the session type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the session type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session from which the analytics were derived */,
  "metric" TEXT /* The type of metric being analyzed (e.g., gait speed, posture score) */,
  "value" REAL /* The calculated value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analytics were computed */,
  "algorithm_used" TEXT /* The specific algorithm or model used for the analytics */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the analytics result */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who requested the analytics */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the analytics entry that triggered the alert */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who should be notified about the alert */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient (if applicable) */,
  "action_required" TEXT /* Action required to address the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_analytics_id FOREIGN KEY ("analytics_id") REFERENCES analytics ("analytics_id")
)
